subject: Anthropology
title: Denisovans
description: A micro-lesson that makes Denisovans usable.

hook.text: A single finger bone in a Siberian cave revealed an entirely unknown human species. DNA told the whole story.

buildup.text: Denisovans are known mainly from DNA extracted from fragments found in Denisova Cave in Russia's Altai Mountains.

discovery.text: They interbred with both Neanderthals and Homo sapiens. Modern Melanesians carry up to 6% Denisovan DNA.

twist.text: Denisovan genes help Tibetans survive at high altitude. Ancient interbreeding gave us real survival advantages.

climax.text: Denisovans prove that our past is messier than we thought — full of mixing, mingling, and shared genes.

punchline.text: A finger bone rewrote human history.
